name: Nicotine Dependence & Smoking Response
description: Common smoking-heaviness and nicotine-reinforcement markers. Behavioral predisposition only; not deterministic.

snps.description: The strongest common CHRNA5 smoking-dependence marker in many GWAS; associated with heavier smoking among smokers.

GG.note: No common CHRNA5 nicotine-risk allele detected at this site.

AG.note: One CHRNA5 risk allele is present. If nicotine use starts, reinforcement can run stronger than baseline.

AA.note: Two CHRNA5 risk alleles are present. This is the strongest common dependence-prone pattern at this site.

snps.description: Closely related to smoking heaviness and toxin exposure among smokers.

GG.note: No common CHRNA3 smoking-quantity risk allele detected at this site.

AG.note: One CHRNA3 risk allele is present. This can track with heavier smoking once nicotine use begins.

AA.note: Two CHRNA3 risk alleles are present. This is a stronger common signal for heavier smoking exposure.

snps.description: Expression-linked CHRNA5 marker that refines nicotine-dependence tendency when paired with rs16969968.

TT.note: No common rs588765 risk allele detected at this site.

CT.note: One regulatory risk allele is present. This is a smaller signal than rs16969968 but can reinforce it.

CC.note: Two regulatory risk alleles are present. This is a moderate added nicotine-reinforcement signal.
